file_insights.purpose: Main admin dashboard view with statistics, charts, and quick actions
key_features: Stats cards grid (orders, revenue, menus, pending orders)
Revenue overview chart with period selection
Quick actions section with navigation links
Recent orders table with order details

file_insights.purpose: Form for creating new menu items
Form for editing existing menu items
Detailed view of single order with full details and actions
key_features: Order header with number and timestamps
Order items list with prices and quantities

patterns_discovered.description: Extensive use of `locals.variable || locals.variable ? value : default` pattern to safely access potentially undefined template variables
Consistent pattern of checking if `t` function exists and providing English fallback text
patterns_discovered.frequency: Very High - used for all user-facing text

patterns_discovered.description: Multi-breakpoint Tailwind CSS classes for responsive layouts
Consistent use of dark: prefixed classes for dark mode color variants
patterns_discovered.frequency: High - all color-dependent elements have dark variants

patterns_discovered.description: Phosphor Icons (ph) used throughout with aria-hidden for accessibility
patterns_discovered.frequency: Very High - decorative icons on buttons and indicators

patterns_discovered.description: Consistent use of `.card` class for styled containers with padding and borders
patterns_discovered.frequency: High - used for all main content sections

patterns_discovered.description: Dedicated empty state sections with icons and call-to-action buttons
patterns_discovered.example: No orders, no data messages with navigation to relevant pages
patterns_discovered.frequency: Medium - used in dashboard, menus, and orders views

patterns_discovered.description: Dynamic display of trends with color coding (green for positive, red for negative)
Alpine.js/Tailwind hover groups for coordinated hover effects
patterns_discovered.frequency: Medium - quick action cards, table rows

patterns_discovered.description: x-data for period selection and other simple state management
patterns_discovered.frequency: Low - minimal JS usage, mostly CSS-driven

gotchas_discovered.description: Heavy reliance on checking `locals.variable` before accessing properties. This is necessary but verbose and could lead to errors if developers forget the check.
gotchas_discovered.example: Must write `locals.stats ? stats.property : 0` instead of just `stats.property`

gotchas_discovered.description: Every user-facing string requires verbose `typeof t === 'function' ? t('key') : 'fallback'` pattern, creating substantial template bloat
gotchas_discovered.example: Simple text like 'Orders Today' requires 3 lines of code

gotchas_discovered.description: Turkish Lira (TRY) is hardcoded in revenue formatting, reducing flexibility for multi-currency support
Hiding elements on mobile with hidden sm:table-cell creates maintenance burden - content must exist in DOM even when hidden
gotchas_discovered.example: Customer names shown in different places for mobile vs desktop

gotchas_discovered.description: Chart rendering logic assumes canvas element and external charting library, but no clear indication of which library is used
gotchas_discovered.example: <canvas id="revenueChart" class="w-full h-full"></canvas> - no library initialized in template

gotchas_discovered.description: Status color mapping defined inline in forEach loop rather than as a helper, creating potential for inconsistency
gotchas_discovered.example: statusColors object recreated for each order in table

gotchas_discovered.description: Quick action links and navigation assume certain routes exist (/admin/menus/create, /admin/orders, etc) with no 404 fallback
gotchas_discovered.example: href="/admin/menus/create" - assumes route exists and user has permission

gotchas_discovered.description: Excessive use of aria-hidden="true" on icons, while correct for decorative icons, requires trust that all icon usage is truly decorative
gotchas_discovered.example: All Phosphor icons marked as aria-hidden without verification

approach_outcome.implementation_scope: Complete admin dashboard views created with dashboard overview, menu management, and order management interfaces
key_accomplishments: Dashboard view with KPI cards, revenue chart, quick actions, and recent orders list
Menu management views (index, create, edit) with full CRUD forms
Order management views (index, detail) with filtering and status tracking
Responsive design supporting mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
Dark mode support across all views
Empty state handling with call-to-action buttons
Consistent styling using design system tokens
design_decisions: Tailwind CSS for styling with responsive utilities
Dark mode variant for every color usage
Defensive coding with locals checks for template variables
Comprehensive i18n support with fallback text

recommendations.suggestion: Create EJS helper functions or partials to reduce boilerplate
recommendations.detail: Move repeated patterns like i18n ternaries and locals checks into reusable helpers to improve maintainability and reduce template size

recommendations.suggestion: Implement i18n wrapper function at template level
recommendations.detail: Create a template helper that handles the `typeof t === 'function'` check once, reducing verbosity throughout templates
Move hardcoded values like currency ('TRY'), locale ('tr-TR'), and color mappings to configuration files for easier customization
Add comments indicating which charting library is expected (Chart.js, D3.js, etc.) and how it should be initialized for the canvas element

recommendations.suggestion: Extract status badge, stat card, and quick action components as partials
recommendations.detail: Create reusable EJS partials for frequently repeated patterns (status badges, stat cards, action buttons) to improve consistency and maintainability

recommendations.suggestion: Add ARIA labels to interactive elements
recommendations.detail: Buttons and links should have descriptive aria-labels in addition to visible text for screen reader users
Include conditional blocks for displaying validation errors from form submissions in create/edit views
If recent orders list grows large, implement pagination or virtualization to maintain performance
Develop test data fixtures for all expected data structures (stats, orders, menus) to enable consistent testing
